title: benchmarking updates for Glassbox
summary:
Spent last few days fixing issues with the benchmark pipeline. Previously, the engine would hang or overfit on easy problems. Now I added deterministic seeding so multiple runs are consistent. Also implemented an adaptive compute budget that scales based on problem difficulty.

Added native support for integer powers and explicit division in the C++ engine. This helps recover rational functions better. I also added a greedy noise reduction step using BIC to prune unnecessary terms. Finally, added native timeouts through OpenMP to stop infinite loops. Everything passed smoke tests, and the multi-start protocol now matches the standard SRBench format.
commitMessages: feat(srbench): enhance formula evaluation and timeout management with adaptive options
feat(srbench): implement robust fast-path noise reduction and C++ timeouts
